If BlasInt is Int32, it will throw an error. I assume the authors meant info::BlasInt = convert(BlasInt,0) or something. The statement above only works if BlasInt is Int64 (or you are running on 32 bit system). cpus"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2024-10-11T10:14:43Z","updated_at":"2024-11-13T21:28:40Z","closed_at":"2024-10-16T06:06:42Z","author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56105","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56105","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56105.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56105.patch","merged_at":"2024-10-16T06:06:42Z"},"body":"Rebase and extension of @alexfanqi's initial work on porting Julia to RISC-V. Requires LLVM 19.

Tested on a VisionFive2, built with:

```make
MARCH := rv64gc_zba_zbb
MCPU := sifive-u74

USE_BINARYBUILDER:=0

DEPS_GIT = llvm
override LLVM_VER=19.1.1
override LLVM_BRANCH=julia-release/19.x
override LLVM_SHA1=julia-release/19.x
```

In case anybody wants to test this out, I'll be uploading builds here: https://github.com/maleadt/julia/releases

```julia-repl
❯ ./julia
               _
   _       _ _(_)_     |  Documentation: https://docs.julialang.org
  (_)     | (_) (_)    |
   _ _   _| |_  __ _   |  Type "?" for help, "]?" for Pkg help.
  | | | | | | |/ _` |  |
  | | |_| | | | (_| |  |  Version 1.12.0-DEV.1374 (2024-10-14)
 _/ |\__'_|_|_|\__'_|  |  riscv/25092a3982* (fork: 1 commits, 0 days)
|__/                   |

julia> versioninfo(; verbose=true)
Julia Version 1.12.0-DEV.1374
Commit 25092a3982* (2024-10-14 09:57 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (riscv64-unknown-linux-gnu)
  uname: Linux 6.11.3-1-riscv64 #1 SMP Debian 6.11.3-1 (2024-10-10) riscv64 unknown
  CPU: unknown:
              speed         user         nice          sys         idle          irq
       #1  1500 MHz        922 s          0 s        265 s     160953 s          0 s
       #2  1500 MHz        457 s          0 s        280 s     161521 s          0 s
       #3  1500 MHz        452 s          0 s        270 s     160911 s          0 s
       #4  1500 MHz        638 s         15 s        301 s     161340 s          0 s
  Memory: 7.760246276855469 GB (7474.08203125 MB free)
  Uptime: 16260.13 sec
  Load Avg:  0.25  0.23  0.1
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LLVM: libLLVM-19.1.1 (ORCJIT, sifive-u74)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 4 virtual cores)
Environment:
  HOME = /home/tim
  PATH = /home/tim/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/games
  TERM = xterm-256color


julia> ccall(:jl_dump_host_cpu, Nothing, ())
CPU: sifive-u74
Features: +zbb,+d,+i,+f,+c,+a,+zba,+m,-zvbc,-zksed,-zvfhmin,-zbkc,-zkne,-zksh,-zfh,-zfhmin,-zknh,-v,-zihintpause,-zicboz,-zbs,-zvknha,-zvksed,-zfa,-ztso,-zbc,-zvknhb,-zihintntl,-zknd,-zvbb,-zbkx,-zkt,-zvkt,-zicond,-zvksh,-zvfh,-zvkg,-zvkb,-zbkb,-zvkned


julia> @code_native debuginfo=:none 1+2.
	.text
	.attribute	4, 16
	.attribute	5, "rv64i2p1_m2p0_a2p1_f2p2_d2p2_c2p0_zicsr2p0_zifencei2p0_zmmul1p0_zba1p0_zbb1p0"
	.file	"+"
	.globl	"julia_+_3003"
	.p2align	1
	.type	"julia_+_3003",@function
"julia_+_3003":
	addi	sp, sp, -16
	sd	ra, 8(sp)
	sd	s0, 0(sp)
	addi	s0, sp, 16
	fcvt.d.l	fa5, a0
	ld	ra, 8(sp)
	ld	s0, 0(sp)
	fadd.d	fa0, fa5, fa0
	addi	sp, sp, 16
	ret
.Lfunc_end0:
	.size	"julia_+_3003", .Lfunc_end0-"julia_+_3003"

	.type	".L+Core.Float64#3005",@object
	.section	.data.rel.ro,"aw",@progbits
	.p2align	3, 0x0
".L+Core.Float64#3005":
	.quad	".L+Core.Float64#3005.jit"
	.size	".L+Core.Float64#3005", 8

.set ".L+Core.Float64#3005.jit", 272467692544
	.size	".L+Core.Float64#3005.jit", 8
	.section	".note.GNU-stack","",@progbits
```

Lots of bugs guaranteed, but with this we at least have a functional build and REPL for further development by whoever is interested 🙂 

Probably also requires Linux 6.4+, since the fallback processor detection used here relies on LLVM's `sys::getHostCPUFeatures`, which for RISC-V is implemented using hwprobe introduced in 6.4. We could probably add a fallback that parses `/proc/cpuinfo`, either by building a CPU database much like how we've done for AArch64, or by parsing the actual ISA string contained there. That would probably also be a good place to add support for profiles, which are supposedly the way forward to package RISC-V binaries. That can happen in follow-up PRs though.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56105/reactions","total_count":21,"+1":6,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":9,"confused":0,"heart":4,"rocket":2,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56105/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2474823074","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56105#issuecomment-2474823074","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56105","id":2474823074,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Tgs2i","user":{"login":"stensmo","id":141365386,"node_id":"U_kgDOCG0Qig","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/141365386?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo","html_url":"https://github.com/stensmo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-13T21:28:39Z","updated_at":"2024-11-13T21:28:39Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I tested the build v1.12-aba4401f73 and it works really well. The issue relates to negative NaN and positive Nan. Why is it so important that the sign is correct? In that case:

On X86 
julia> 0.0*Inf64===-NaN64
true

On Risc-V
julia> 0.0*Inf64===NaN64
true

Why does X86 provide the negative Nan? If you only look at the type and use isnan, everything works as expected on all platforms. In that case, the tests can be modified.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

testing https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/94f542dcd2f67aa066fc17b08904125facc12c11/test/numbers.jl#L2917-L2930 some tests related to NaN are failing. The issue relates to negative NaN and positive Nan. Why is it so important that the sign is correct? In that case:

On X86
```julia-repl
julia> 0.0*Inf64===-NaN64
true
```
On Risc-V
```julia-repl
julia> 0.0*Inf64===NaN64
true
```
Why does X86 provide the negative Nan? If you only look at the type and use isnan, everything works as expected on all platforms. In that case, the tests can be modified.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2496922703","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672#issuecomment-2496922703","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672","id":2496922703,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6U1ARP","user":{"login":"stensmo","id":141365386,"node_id":"U_kgDOCG0Qig","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/141365386?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo","html_url":"https://github.com/stensmo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-25T05:51:31Z","updated_at":"2024-11-25T05:51:31Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> yeah the issue is that I'm pretty sure that preserving nan payloads is explicitly not guaranteed by LLVM

Here it says that it wont,  [Behavior of Floating-Point NaN values](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#id1985)](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#floatnan)

Floating-point math operations that return a NaN are an exception from the general principle that LLVM implements IEEE-754 semantics. Unless specified otherwise, the following rules apply whenever the IEEE-754 semantics say that a NaN value is returned: *the result has a non-deterministic sign*; the quiet bit and payload are non-deterministically chosen from the following set of options:","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2496922703/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

functions"},{"id":6857349323,"node_id":"LA_kwDOABkWpM8AAAABmLrYyw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/system:riscv","name":"system:riscv","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":"Support for Risc-V cpus"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-11-24T16:19:25Z","updated_at":"2024-11-26T05:09:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"While testing https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/94f542dcd2f67aa066fc17b08904125facc12c11/test/numbers.jl#L2917-L2930 some tests related to NaN are failing. The issue relates to negative NaN and positive Nan. Why is it so important that the sign is correct? In that case:

On X86
```julia-repl
julia> 0.0*Inf64===-NaN64
true
```
On Risc-V
```julia-repl
julia> 0.0*Inf64===NaN64
true
```
Why does X86 provide the negative Nan? If you only look at the type and use isnan, everything works as expected on all platforms. In that case, the tests can be modified.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2499673171","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672#issuecomment-2499673171","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56672","id":2499673171,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6U_fxT","user":{"login":"stensmo","id":141365386,"node_id":"U_kgDOCG0Qig","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/141365386?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo","html_url":"https://github.com/stensmo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-26T05:09:36Z","updated_at":"2024-11-26T05:09:36Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Julia must have the exact same semantics as LLVM or as a patched version of LLVM. Here is a case which is not very common: 

df."Sales of 32\" Monitors"=1:4

The correct syntax should be: 

 df.var"Sales of 32\" Monitors"




As a bonus, if autocomplete could do this it would be great

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51028","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51028/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51028/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51028/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51028","id":1863974677,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5vGf8V","number":51028,"title":"Support Thousands Separator for printf","user":{"login":"stensmo","id":141365386,"node_id":"U_kgDOCG0Qig","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/141365386?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo","html_url":"https://github.com/stensmo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stensmo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-08-23T20:35:17Z","updated_at":"2023-08-23T20:35:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Many packages use printf. There is no support for a thousands separator in the format string.

https://programming-idioms.org/idiom/173/format-a-number-with-grouped-thousands/2431/c

Supporting ' as thousands separator in printf strings would make all packages using printf support it to.
